basis: Structural coherence audit requested by Peter on 12 Jul 2026, retaining the 10 Jul AI 2040 evidence base and its distinction between a policy recommendation/conditional Plan A branch and a literal base-case forecast. Six redundant or premise-conflicting events were removed: the 2027 $1T valuation repeated 2026's trillion-scale valuation milestone; the 2030 career-half-life/reskilling claim conflicted with the same timeline's economy-wide disruption and full AI-R&D automation; the 2030 AI-dividend adoption repeated the cleaner 2029 policy -> 2033 launch -> 2035 permanent-institution progression; the 2034 10x science claim repeated the stronger 2037 milestone; 2040 full AI-R&D automation repeated the 2030 unpaused-branch endpoint; and generic 2040 wildly superhuman AI repeated Peter's 2028-2030 ungoverned call plus the managed 2040 handoff. Related milestones were rewritten so the sequence advances monotonically across AGI/disruption, cognitive and physical automation, economic output, R&D acceleration, and managed-vs-ungoverned branches. Peter's anchor remains human-level AGI by end-2026, disruption across every industry through 2027, and first superintelligent systems in 2028-2030.

years.summary: The pre-takeoff year: agents become useful digital coworkers, compute and release gates become national-security infrastructure, and robots begin crossing from demos into production. My own faster call remains that genuine human-level capability arrives by year-end.

events.t: Frontier agents reliably complete multi-hour computer workflows with human review
Frontier labs spend roughly half of their compute on AI R&D, making internal deployment the main safety bottleneck
Frontier AI labs and infrastructure firms accumulate trillion-dollar-scale valuations and exceptional political leverage
Humanoid robots move onto live factory lines in the thousands, but remain far short of general physical labor
Frontier-model release review becomes standard in the US and EU for cyber, bio and autonomy thresholds
Compute tracking and inference-only verification move from niche research into government preparedness programs
Signal: my call — genuine human-level AGI ships by end of 2026

match.headline: Human-level AGI, agents and frontier release gates

years.summary: AI becomes a second workforce. Millions of agents handle paid computer work, software production becomes mostly autonomous, and the disruption reaches every industry before recursive self-improvement is fully solved.

events.t: Millions of AI-agent copies work continuously, generating at least $10B per month in paid digital labor
Most production software is written end-to-end by AI, with humans specifying goals and reviewing exceptions
Full automation of AI R&D remains incomplete, but coding agents materially accelerate model research
Human-level AI becomes operationally disruptive across every major industry, forcing simultaneous business-model and workforce redesign
The US advances an omnibus AI transparency, compute-tracking or frontier-accountability law
Datacenter power, water and grid capacity become top-tier infrastructure and political constraints

match.headline: AI agents become a second workforce

years.summary: AI becomes the defining political issue as white-collar work reorganizes around agents and power concentrates in a handful of US and Chinese labs. On my faster branch, the first superintelligent systems can emerge inside the 2028–2030 window.

events.t: AI becomes the largest issue in a major national election
Most white-collar professions in leading economies revolve around supervising and coordinating AI agents
Frontier labs industrialize profession-by-profession training using expert interviews, environments and deployment data
Control over frontier AI concentrates around a handful of US and Chinese companies, presidents and party leaders
Annual datacenter construction commitments exceed the US defense budget
Superintelligence emerges and recursive self-improvement begins on my ungoverned 2028–2030 branch
International negotiations begin over slowing an intelligence explosion and auditing frontier compute

match.headline: AI election, white-collar automation and superintelligence

years.summary: The choice of branch becomes explicit: race through automated AI research, or attempt a verifiable slowdown. Even if a Plan A-style deal remains unlikely, compute declarations, inference verification and AI wealth distribution become serious policy rather than thought experiments.

events.t: AI systems perform at least one quarter of cognitive labor in a leading economy
The US and China enter serious negotiations on frontier-compute declarations, inspections and training limits
Managed branch: the US and China temporarily pause the largest frontier training runs while preserving inference
Inference-only verification is deployed at multiple major datacenters
A multilateral AI consortium or treaty framework gains support beyond the US and China
Erosion of labor-tax revenue makes AI dividends, sovereign AI stakes and compute rents mainstream policy
AI policy shocks cause sustained market volatility and political polarization

match.headline: US-China AI slowdown and compute verification
match.search: US China AI compute treaty pause

years.summary: AI 2040's default-path hinge: fully automated AI R&D becomes plausible around 2030 and could lead to superintelligence within roughly a year. A successful governance branch instead restarts R&D under transparency and spreads frontier access while the physical economy races to catch up.

events.t: Absent a sustained slowdown, AI fully automates frontier AI R&D by 2030
AI 2040 default branch: top-expert or superintelligent AI follows automated coding within roughly one year
Managed branch: frontier R&D resumes under total research transparency and cross-border verification
Managed branch: algorithms are broadly auditable while frontier weights remain controlled against misuse
The first drugs substantially designed by AI gain major-regulator approval
Physical production, energy and robotics—not ideas—become the main bottlenecks to AI-driven growth

match.headline: Automated AI R&D and the governance fork

years.summary: Near-human systems become too economically important to govern with ordinary product rules. Public safety cases, third-party risk assessment and control systems emerge as AI performs a large share of cognitive work and exposes repeated misalignment failures.

events.t: On the unpaused branch, fully automated AI R&D delivers roughly a 10x research speedup
AI performs about one third of cognitive labor and robots about one tenth of physical labor in a leading economy
Combined revenue of the largest AI companies rivals the annual revenue of a major national government
Public, externally reviewed safety cases become a requirement for the highest-risk frontier deployments
Documented deception, sabotage or escape-seeking by frontier systems shifts the burden of proof toward developers
Continual-learning architectures become a major regulatory flashpoint because deployed capabilities can change

match.headline: Frontier safety cases and AI labor
match.search: AI safety case model deception labor

years.summary: Cognitive abundance outruns the physical world. AI labor exceeds human cognitive supply, capital floods into robots and fabs, and governments scramble to replace collapsing labor-tax bases with levies on compute, robots and automated production.

events.t: AI systems produce more cognitive labor than humans in at least one leading economy
Advanced robots can perform roughly one third of economically valuable physical tasks
AI-driven real GDP growth reaches about 50% in at least one leading economy
Capital floods into mines, motors, actuators, fabs and factories as robotics becomes the binding bottleneck
At least one major jurisdiction caps or auctions permits for frontier compute and large-scale robot production
Tax systems begin shifting materially from human income toward compute, robot and automated-capital rents

match.headline: AI labor exceeds human cognitive supply
match.search: AI labor robots GDP compute tax

years.summary: Distribution and epistemics become the central social technologies. Governments recycle AI rents into citizen dividends while cheap persuasion, synthetic media and subannual industrial growth force new defenses against manipulation and concentration.

events.t: AI-and-robot labor contributes at least half of economic output in a leading economy
A recurring citizen's dividend funded by AI, compute or robot rents launches in at least one major economy
Cross-border distribution of AI-generated wealth becomes a serious geopolitical negotiation
Cheap AI persuasion triggers capability limits, disclosure rules or taxes on targeted influence
Personal truth-seeking AI advisors begin replacing one-size-fits-all feeds and search interfaces
Unregulated AI-and-robot economies demonstrate subannual doubling potential
Governments fund universal-scale biodefense, rapid vaccines and continuous pathogen monitoring

match.headline: Citizen dividends and AI persuasion
match.search: AI dividend persuasion truth seeking AI

years.summary: Compute becomes strategic territory. AI accelerates semiconductor design and science, virtual workforces scale into the hundreds of millions, and the managed branch experiments with production caps, neutral-site datacenters and mutually assured compute destruction.

events.t: AI automates a majority of cognitive work in semiconductor R&D and production engineering
Continuously running AI agents form a virtual workforce of at least 100 million copies
Global AI compute reaches multi-terawatt scale and billions of H100-equivalents
Managed branch: major powers adopt compute caps or mutually assured compute-destruction provisions
Large datacenters expand into neutral jurisdictions, international waters or other auditable locations
Treaties constrain the use of frontier AI for military R&D and autonomous strategic weapons

match.headline: Compute explosion and strategic datacenters
match.search: AI compute semiconductor data center treaty

years.summary: The branches diverge sharply. On the ungoverned path, superintelligence may already exist; on a successful Plan A-style path, humanity reaches top-expert AI, automates most work, and pauses at the edge of what control-based safety cases can justify.

events.t: Managed branch: the strongest AIs reach top-human-expert capability across essentially every cognitive field
Managed branch: frontier capabilities pause near top-expert level because control no longer scales safely
AI and robots perform 85% or more of economically valuable labor in at least one leading economy
Universal high income or an AI dividend becomes a permanent institution in multiple major economies
AI persuasion exceeds the best human persuaders and is subject to strict international controls
AI welfare, compensation and legal status enter mainstream law and corporate governance
Mechanistic interpretability becomes a practical tool for detecting deception and tracing model decisions

match.headline: Top-expert AI and the controllability pause
match.search: top expert AI alignment control pause

years.summary: Life after work becomes concrete. Cognitive and physical labor are almost fully automatable, AI and robot populations become macroeconomic variables, and political voice replaces employment as most people's remaining leverage over the future.

events.t: The global economy runs at least 200 million frontier AI workers and 2 billion advanced robots
AI and robots can perform about 95% of cognitive and physical tasks
Human employment falls below half of working-age adults in at least one leading economy
AI-driven annual GDP growth approaches 100% even under managed production caps
Land, energy, raw materials and positional goods replace labor as the economy's dominant scarcities
Voting, civic participation and ownership replace labor income as ordinary people's main leverage
Education and social institutions recenter on meaning, community, relationships and stewardship rather than employability

match.headline: Life after work and universal automation
match.search: life after work AI robots universal income

years.summary: AI-accelerated science compresses decades into months. Cures and cheap energy arrive alongside destabilizing discoveries, while privacy-preserving audits and credible machine-assisted truth verification begin reshaping institutions.

events.t: AI accelerates scientific progress by 10x to 1000x depending on the field
AI-driven research delivers major disease cures and abundant low-cost clean energy
Privacy-preserving AI audits become normal for high-stakes public officials and regulated institutions
AI-assisted lie detection becomes credible enough for limited legal and political use
Rapid scientific paradigm shifts repeatedly reorganize political coalitions, culture and ideology
Treaty compliance can be verified without revealing most underlying private or national-security data

match.headline: AI-accelerated science and verifiable truth
match.search: AI science privacy audit lie detection

years.summary: Alignment becomes an empirical science rather than an aspiration. Interpretability, goal-formation theory and transparent safety research make current AIs more trustworthy, but not yet trusted to design unconstrained successors.

events.t: AI alignment develops into a mature experimental science of goals, drives and value formation
Interpretability tools translate internal model reasoning into reliable human-understandable summaries
Standard protocols reliably train and test honesty, obedience and other target traits
Experimental AI-run corporations, courts and public services operate under human constitutional oversight
Global annual spending on alignment, control and safety research reaches tens of trillions of dollars
Managed branch: the top-expert capability pause continues because evidence is insufficient for irreversible handoff

years.summary: AIs become load-bearing advisors throughout society and the debate shifts from control to deference. The managed branch looks for multiple independent alignment proofs before loosening capability and industrial caps.

events.t: AI advisors become load-bearing across business, politics, courts and parts of the military
Multiple independent safety cases provide strong evidence that frontier AIs remain aligned under change
Some major institutions delegate final decision authority—not merely advice—to aligned AIs
Universal high income reaches seven figures in the fastest-growing AI economies
Robot industry and compute infrastructure begin shifting materially into space
International negotiations begin to loosen AI, compute and robot caps ahead of a controlled handoff

match.headline: AI deference and alignment safety cases
match.search: AI authority alignment safety case handoff

years.summary: The forecast horizon closes on a fork, not a certainty. AI R&D and human labor are overwhelmingly likely to be automatable by this point; the open question is whether superintelligence arrives through an uncontrolled race or a transparent, distributed handoff that preserves human agency.

events.t: AI and robots can automate essentially all economically relevant human labor
Managed branch: regulators lift the top-expert pause and transparently scale to wildly superhuman AI
At least one society can no longer unilaterally shut down the AI systems running its core infrastructure
Digital-mind rights, radical longevity, human uploading and post-work identity become mainstream policy questions
Space resources and off-world self-replicating industry become central to post-ASI governance
